channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Specially minted Paul McCartney UK coins to go on sale as part of legends collection
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Beatles legend Paul McCartney is being honored in a specially minted British coin collection.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: An Ole Miss student exchanged messages with the man now on trial in his killing, police say
item.description: In the final hours before University of Mississippi student Jimmy “Jay” Lee disappeared, sexually explicit Snapchat messages were exchanged between his account and the account of the man now on trial in his killing, an investigator testified Thursday. Sheldon “Timothy” Herrington Jr.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House rejects Democratic efforts to force release of Matt Gaetz ethics report
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House shut down Democrats' efforts Thursday to release the long-awaited ethics report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz, pushing the fate of any resolution to the yearslong investigation of sexual misconduct allegations into further uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Police test DNA and fingerprints on discarded bottle as they hunt for UnitedHealthcare CEO's killer
item.description: The masked gunman who killed the leader of one of the largest U.S. health insurance companies in front of midtown Manhattan surveillance cameras remained at large Thursday as the nation’s largest police department hunted for him.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US judge rejects Boeing's plea deal in a conspiracy case stemming from fatal plane crashes
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday rejected a deal that would have let Boeing plead guilty to a felony conspiracy charge and pay a fine for misleading U.S. regulators about the 737 Max jetliner before two of the planes crashed, killing 346 people. U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: QVC recalls 1.1 million oven gloves after more than 90 burn injury reports
item.description: QVC is recalling more than 1 million pairs of oven gloves after dozens of people were burned while using them, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EPA hails 'revitalized' enforcement efforts as Biden administration heads to exit
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Environmental Protection Agency enhanced enforcement efforts this year, doubling financial penalties issued to polluters and issuing the first-ever arrest for a climate change-related crime, the agency said in a report Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Quake prompts brief tsunami warning on the West Coast. Here's what to know about tsunamis
item.description: The powerful earthquake that struck in Northern California on Thursday prompted a brief tsunami warning affecting about 5 million people along a stretch of the West Coast — from Northern California to Oregon — before being canceled.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Shouting match erupts over 9/11 during Secret Service hearing
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe and Rep. Pat Fallon (R-Texas) engaged in a shouting match that persisted over gavel banging, as Rowe accused the lawmaker of politicizing the Sept. 11 attack.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Secret Service leader, GOP rep get into shouting match over 9/11 questioning
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe and Rep. Pat Fallon (R-Texas) engaged in a shouting match that persisted over gavel banging, as Rowe accused the lawmaker of politicizing the Sept. 11 attack.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NASA announces further delays to Artemis moon missions
item.description: NASA is preparing to hold a news conference to discuss the agency’s Artemis program, which concerns its plans for moon launches in coming years.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, KRON4

item.title: Dollar Tree could change prices, remove merchandise amid Trump's tariff plan
item.description: Dollar Tree announced they are ready to make certain changes amid President-elect Donald Trump's tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Madie MacDonald, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield reverses decision to cap anesthesia coverage after a set time limit
item.description: Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield said in a statement that its decision to backpedal resulted from “significant widespread misinformation” about the policy.
item.dc:creator: Melanie Higgins, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congo government 'on alert' over mystery flu-like disease that killed dozens
item.description: “First diagnostics are leading us to think it is a respiratory disease,” an official said. “But we need to wait for the laboratory results.
item.dc:creator: Jean-Yves Kamale, KRON4

item.title: Amnesty International says Israel committing genocide in Gaza. Israel rejects allegations
item.description: The offensive is among the deadliest and most destructive since World War II, and has destroyed vast areas of the besieged coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bitcoin hits $100,000 after Trump names pro-crypto SEC chair
item.description: The former president is bolstering the evolving platform amidst the threat of new currency systems seeking to challenge the U.S. dollar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at California school; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, WGN 9

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son sparks calls for granting more clemencies
item.description: President Biden’s surprise pardon of his son Hunter has prompted calls for the outgoing commander in chief to grant clemency to other Americans before he hands over the White House to President-elect Trump next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Jamie Foxx pushes on Capitol Hill for more Down syndrome research in honor of sister
item.description: The DeOndra Dixon INCLUDE Project Act, named after actor Jamie Foxx’s late sister who had Down syndrome, has already passed the House and been introduced to the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, NewsNation

item.title: Police release new photos as they search for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: Two law enforcement officials say a masked gunman who stalked and killed the leader of one of the largest U_S_ health insurance companies on a Manhattan sidewalk used ammunition emblazoned with the words “deny,” “defend” and “depose.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Karen Matthews And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taylor Swift 'Eras' tie-in book is a smash, selling more than 800,000 copies in first weekend
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just one retail chain was enough for Taylor Swift to have the top-selling book last week and the biggest publishing launch of 2024. According to Circana, Swift's “Eras Tour Book” sold 814,000 copies over the Thanksgiving holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Is it hotter than the sun outside? Wacky weather app bug says it is
item.description: If you took a look at the weather widget on your Windows computer Wednesday afternoon, you may have noticed that the numbers were higher than normal for winter.
item.dc:creator: Joey Rather, Fox 5

item.title: Memphis police use excessive force and discriminate against Black people, Justice Department finds
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — The Memphis Police Department uses excessive force and discriminates against Black people, according to the findings of a U.S. Department of Justice investigation launched after the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, JONATHAN MATTISE and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two boys, ages 5 and 6, shot at religious school in Northern California; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, NewsNation

item.title: Judge considers first lawsuit to overturn Missouri's near-total abortion ban
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Abortion-rights advocates on Wednesday asked a judge to overturn Missouri’s near-total ban on the procedure, less than a month after voters backed an abortion-rights constitutional amendment. Jackson County Circuit Judge Jerri Zhang did not immediately rule after the hearing.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California Christian school shooting wounds 2 boys, 5 and 6; suspected gunman dead
item.description: Two California private school students, ages 5 and 6, were shot Wednesday, and the suspected gunman is dead, according to authorities and reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth nomination sinking fast in the Senate
item.description: Senate Republicans say Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s choice to head the Defense Department, faces a very tough path to confirmation in the Senate and his bleak prospects have been communicated directly to the Trump transition team, which is now mulling other options.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at California school; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, Fox 5

item.title: Jury revisits key videos in NYC subway chokehold death trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jurors asked Wednesday to review police and bystander video at the heart of the chokehold manslaughter case against Daniel Penny as his lawyers complained that the Marine veteran was being harassed outside the New York City courthouse.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trooper, the dog abandoned on I-75 before Hurricane Milton, finds forever home
item.description: Trooper, the dog who was rescued by the Florida Highway Patrol during Hurricane Milton has now been adopted, the Leon County Humane Society announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KRON4

item.title: Rescuers search for woman who may have fallen into a sinkhole while looking for her lost cat
item.description: UNITY TOWNSHIP, Pa. (AP) — Rescuers are searching for a woman who went looking for her lost cat and apparently fell into a sinkhole that opened above an abandoned western Pennsylvania coal mine.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and GENE PUSKAR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump may replace Hegseth with DeSantis: WSJ
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is rumored to be considering replacing his Defense Secretary pick, former Fox News personality Pete Hegseth, with Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R).
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: No winner for Mega Millions, jackpot grows to $579 million
item.description: No one won the Mega Millions drawing on Tuesday night. The jackpot has now grown to an estimated $579 million ahead of the next drawing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Knight, WGN 9

item.title: Ex-ballerina convicted of killing husband in 'Black Swan' case gets 20 years in prison
item.description: Ashley Benefield, who was found guilty of manslaughter for shooting her husband during a domestic argument in 2020 sentencing hearing is set for today.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Sinkhole may have swallowed Pennsylvania grandmother looking for missing cat: police
item.description: Authorities fear a Pennsylvania grandmother searching for her missing cat may have been swallowed by a sinkhole.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, WGN 9

item.title: US biathletes fear retaliation for speaking out about sexual harassment, report finds
item.description: U.S. biathletes face “misogynistic” behavior while racing and fear retaliation if they report their concerns, according to an inquiry by an independent contractor hired to assess athlete safety. The investigation of U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Wisconsin's collective bargaining law for public workers
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A judge's overturning of Wisconsin's 13-year-old law that effectively ended collective bargaining for teachers and most state government employees has rekindled a battle over labor rights in a state where the first public sector unions were formed 65 years ago.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4
